title: hymenophyllum tomentosum
intro: Hymenophyllum tomentosum, also known as the hairy filmy fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, Hymenophyllum tomentosum is often used in infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential allergic reactions.
